```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from IPython.display import display
from lets_plot import *

# Make numpy values easier to read.
np.set_printoptions(precision=3, suppress=True)

LetsPlot.setup_html()

bikes = pd.read_csv(
    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byui-cse/cse450-course/master/data/bikes.csv")

bikes["dteday"] = pd.to_datetime(bikes["dteday"], errors="coerce")
bikes["total_rentals"] = bikes["casual"] + bikes["registered"]
bikes["season"] = bikes["season"].astype("category")
bikes["weathersit"] = bikes["weathersit"].astype("category")
bikes["holiday"] = bikes["holiday"].astype("category")
bikes["workingday"] = bikes["workingday"].astype("category")
```


```{python}
bikes.info()
```


## Rental relationships by data type

```{python}
numeric_cols = ["temp_c", "feels_like_c", "hum", "windspeed", "hr"]
for col in numeric_cols:
    p = (
        ggplot(bikes, aes(x=col, y="total_rentals", color="season"))
        + geom_point(alpha=0.25, size=1.2)
        + geom_smooth(method="loess", color="#1f77b4", se=True)
        + labs(
            title=f"Total Rentals vs {col.replace('_', ' ').title()}",
            x=col.replace("_", " ").title(),
            y="Total Rentals",
            color="Season",
        )
        + theme_minimal()
        + theme(axis_text_x=element_text(angle=20, hjust=1))
    )
    display(p)
```

```{python}
cat_cols = ["season", "weathersit", "holiday", "workingday"]
for col in cat_cols:
    p = (
        ggplot(bikes, aes(x=col, y="total_rentals", fill=col))
        + geom_boxplot(color="#2c7fb8")
        + labs(
            title=f"Total Rental Distribution by {col.title()}",
            x=col.replace("_", " ").title(),
            y="Total Rentals",
            fill=col.replace("_", " ").title(),
        )
        + theme_minimal()
        + theme(axis_text_x=element_text(angle=25, hjust=1))
    )
    display(p)
```

## Hourly and seasonal rental patterns

```{python}
hourly = (
    bikes.groupby(["hr", "season"], as_index=False)
    .agg({"total_rentals": "mean"})
    .rename(columns={"total_rentals": "avg_rentals"})
)
p = (
    ggplot(hourly, aes(x="hr", y="avg_rentals", color="season", group="season"))
    + geom_line(size=1.2)
    + geom_point(size=2)
    + labs(
        title="Average Hourly Rentals by Season",
        x="Hour of Day",
        y="Average Total Rentals",
        color="Season",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
)
display(p)
```

```{python}
weather = (
    bikes.groupby(["weathersit", "hr"], as_index=False)
    .agg({"total_rentals": "mean"})
    .rename(columns={"total_rentals": "avg_rentals"})
)
p = (
    ggplot(weather, aes(x="hr", y="avg_rentals", color="weathersit", group="weathersit"))
    + geom_line(size=1.2)
    + labs(
        title="Hourly Rentals by Weather Situation",
        x="Hour of Day",
        y="Average Total Rentals",
        color="Weather Situation",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
)
display(p)
```

## Holiday and workday effects on rental volume

```{python}
season_mean = (
    bikes.groupby("season", as_index=False)
    .agg({"total_rentals": "mean"})
    .rename(columns={"total_rentals": "avg_rentals"})
)
p = (
    ggplot(season_mean, aes(x="season", y="avg_rentals", fill="season"))
    + geom_bar(stat="identity", color="black")
    + labs(
        title="Average Rentals by Season",
        x="Season",
        y="Average Total Rentals",
        fill="Season",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(axis_text_x=element_text(angle=20, hjust=1))
)
display(p)
```

```{python}
holiday_mean = (
    bikes.groupby("holiday", as_index=False)
    .agg({"total_rentals": "mean"})
    .rename(columns={"total_rentals": "avg_rentals"})
)
p = (
    ggplot(holiday_mean, aes(x="holiday", y="avg_rentals", fill="holiday"))
    + geom_bar(stat="identity", color="black")
    + labs(
        title="Average Rentals: Holiday vs Non-Holiday",
        x="Holiday",
        y="Average Total Rentals",
        fill="Holiday",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(axis_text_x=element_text(angle=20, hjust=1))
)
display(p)
```



```{python}
ggplot(bikes, aes(x="dteday", y="registered")) + \
    stat_summary(geom="bar", fun="median")+\
    facet_wrap("holiday")
```
